item_name: OpenDental Appt Not Syncing to GHL
creator: Sohib Boundaoui
body:
NEW BUG — reported by Sohib

Scenario: A contact came into GHL. She called in, and the team manually moved her opportunity status to "Appointment Scheduled." The actual appointment was then booked directly in OpenDental (not through GHL). No appointment ever appeared on the GHL calendar — the OpenDental → GHL sync did not pick it up.

Likely cause: this looks like a DigitalOcean sync miss on the OpenDental → GHL direction (the piece that checks OpenDental every 5 minutes and pushes appointments into the matching doctor's GHL calendar). Possible causes to check: the manual opportunity-stage change may have interfered with the sync's matching logic, the appointment may not have had a valid OpenDental patient ID link, or the sync simply missed/skipped this record.

What's needed: find this specific patient/appointment and confirm it exists correctly in OpenDental, check DigitalOcean sync logs for why it wasn't pushed to GHL, manually push the missing appointment into GHL now so the record is correct, and identify + fix the root cause so this doesn't keep happening when a stage is updated manually ahead of the actual booking.

body:
﻿Salam @Sohib Boundaoui﻿,
﻿
﻿﻿﻿﻿

The Google Ads account has not been performing well, so I went through it in detail today. Here is what I found and what I have done.

﻿

Optimisations made today

-Paused the underperforming keywords that were spending without results.

-Added new high intent keywords around fall detection watches, adult children buying for a parent, and Life Alert comparison searches.

-Increased CPCs on our best performing keywords so they show more often and in better positions.

-Added negative keywords to block irrelevant searches such as Apple Watch, Samsung, Fitbit, free and Medicare related terms.

﻿

The call answering issue

Call tracking is working, but none of the calls are lasting over 60 seconds, which is the minimum length for a call we count as a conversion. Calls this short usually mean one of two things. Either the calls are going unanswered and ringing out, or callers are asking one quick question such as price or Medicare coverage and then hanging up.

The ad schedule is already set on the campaign, so ads only run during the hours we have set. Even so, I can see in GHL that most calls are going unanswered when they first come in, and this is the main reason they are not counting as conversions. If these calls are answered live on the first attempt, call length will go up and conversions will start recording properly.

﻿

We need to raise this concern with the client and suggest them to make sure that calls are answered promptly.

﻿

Further Recommendation

I recommend we add the Google Ads tracking number on the landing page (liferun.com/start). Right now we only track calls made directly from the ads. With the number on the page, anyone who clicks an ad, visits the site and calls from there will also be tracked back to the campaign and keyword. This will not affect Meta reporting in any way, since visitors from the Meta campaigns do not go to this page.

Google Ads Weekly Report

Reporting Period: July 3, 2026 – July 9, 2026

﻿

Overall Performance

The account continued to generate qualified search traffic, with the BWS - Search - Phone Calls campaign driving the majority of activity and producing 14 phone call leads during the reporting period. The account maintained a healthy click-through rate while focusing spend on users actively searching for HVAC services.

During the week, several optimizations were implemented to improve traffic quality and budget efficiency. The secondary campaign, AC Installation and Replacement, was paused to concentrate budget on the stronger-performing phone call campaign, while extensive negative keyword refinements were introduced to reduce irrelevant searches.

﻿

Impressions: 527

Average CTR: 7.21%

Phone Calls: 14

﻿

Campaign Performance

BWS - Search - Phone Calls

The Search - Phone Calls campaign remained the primary source of leads throughout the reporting period.

Amount Spent: $229.66

Clicks: 36

Impressions: 499

CTR: 7.21%

Average CPC: $6.38

Phone Calls: 14

The campaign continued to generate a consistent flow of inbound phone enquiries while maintaining a strong click-through rate. With nearly all account spend allocated to this campaign, it remains the primary driver of lead generation and continues to attract high-intent search traffic.

AC Installation and Replacement

The AC Installation and Replacement campaign received limited traffic before being paused during the reporting period.

Amount Spent: $24.57

Clicks: 2

Impressions: 28

CTR: 7.14%

Average CPC: $12.29

The campaign generated minimal activity before being paused to prioritize budget toward the higher-performing phone call campaign. Resources will now be concentrated on the campaign producing the strongest lead volume.

﻿

Optimizations Completed This Week

Several optimizations were completed to improve campaign efficiency and traffic quality:

  • Reduced the daily budget for the BWS - Search - Phone Calls campaign from $40/day to $20/day, and subsequently to $15/day, to better align spend with the current strategy.
  • Paused the AC Installation and Replacement campaign to focus budget on the stronger-performing phone call campaign.
  • Added 33 negative phrase match keywords across both campaigns to exclude searches related to competitor businesses and irrelevant HVAC providers.
  • Added 19 location-based negative broad match keywords to prevent ads from appearing in searches related to cities and regions outside the target service area.

These changes were implemented to reduce wasted ad spend, improve traffic quality, and ensure the budget is focused on the most relevant, high-intent searches.

﻿

Overall Summary

The account generated 14 phone call leads while maintaining a 7.21% click-through rate across search campaigns. Strategic optimizations during the week included budget adjustments, campaign consolidation, and extensive negative keyword additions to improve targeting and eliminate irrelevant traffic. Going forward, performance will continue to be monitored closely to evaluate the impact of these changes and identify additional opportunities to increase lead volume while maintaining cost efficiency.

body:
Quick update, since the last budget change and the numbers haven't recovered ($426 spent, 8 purchases at 2.67 ROAS vs 10 at 5.07 the week before). 

﻿I would recommend the following changes.

﻿

1. Add frequency cap on the retargeting campaigns. The retargeting audience is still to small, which is why frequency in both retargeting ad sets is very high.. I ﻿would suggest capping impressions at 2 per 7 days this will decrease the spending while still fulfilling the purpose of retargeting campaign.

﻿

2. Individual product ads alongside the subscriptions. Signing up for a monthly subscription is a big ask for someone who's never bought from you before.
﻿﻿Most people want to try the product first before committing to something recurring.
﻿So, I would recommend that we run some top sellers individually, this way we can bring first-time buyers in and then we can bring them back to the subscription later through retargeting.
﻿This will require creating some new creatives even if statics.
﻿
﻿Please let me know your thoughts on this.
